dhp360:1: Restraint with the eye is good,
dhp360:2: good is restraint with the ear.
dhp360:3: Restraint with the nose is good,
dhp360:4:
good is restraint with the tongue.
Restraint with the body is good, good is restraint with speech.
Restraint with the heart is good, good is restraint everywhere.
A monk everywhere restrained is released from all suffering & stress.
dhp362:4:
content, centered, alone:
he’s what they call a monk.
dhp363:1: A monk restrained in his speaking,
dhp364:1: Dhamma his dwelling, Dhamma his delight,
dhp364:4: does not fall away from true Dhamma.
dhp365:1:
Gains:
don’t treat your own with scorn,
dhp365:2: don’t go coveting those of others.
dhp365:3: A monk who covets those of others
dhp366:1: Even if he gets next to nothing,
dhp366:2:
he doesn’t treat his gains with scorn.
Living purely, untiring:
dhp366:3: he’s the one that the devas praise.
dhp367:1: For whom, in name & form in every way,
dhp367:3: & who doesn’t grieve for what’s not:
dhp368:2: with faith in the Awakened One’s teaching,
dhp368:3: would attain the good state, the peaceful state:
dhp369:2: It will take you lightly when bailed.
dhp369:4: you go from there to Unbinding.
dhp370:1: Cut through five, let go of five,
dhp370:3: A monk gone past five attachments
dhp370:4: is said to have crossed the flood.
dhp371:1: Practice jhana, monk, and don’t be heedless.
dhp371:2: Don’t take your mind roaming in sensual strands.
dhp371:3: Don’t swallow–heedless– the ball of iron aflame.
dhp372:1: There’s no jhana for one with no discernment,
dhp372:2: no discernment for one with no jhana.
dhp372:3: But one with both jhana & discernment:
dhp372:4: he’s on the verge of Unbinding.
dhp373:1: A monk with his mind at peace, going into an empty dwelling, clearly seeing the Dhamma aright:
dhp373:3: his delight is more than human.
dhp374:1: However it is, however it is he touches
dhp374:4: that, for those who know it, is deathless, the Deathless.
dhp375:4:
restraint in line with the Patimokkha.
He should associate with admirable friends.
dhp376:6: he will put an end to suffering & stress.
dhp378:1: Calmed in body, calmed in speech,
dhp378:3: having disgorged the baits of the world,
dhp378:4: a monk is called thoroughly calmed.
dhp379:3: As a self-guarded monk with guarded self,
dhp380:1: Your own self is your own mainstay.
dhp380:3: Your own self is your own guide.
dhp380:4: Therefore you should watch over yourself–
dhp380:5: as a trader, a fine steed.
dhp381:1: A monk with a manifold joy,
dhp381:2: with faith in the Awakened One’s teaching,
dhp382:4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
